html:
[1250]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蛇妖 - Compound term meaning 'snake demon' or 'snake spirit,' referring to a supernatural being in the form of a snake.
  • 化形魅人 - Idiomatic expression describing the ability of a snake to change its form and enchant humans.
  • 真形 - Refers to the true, original form of the snake, as opposed to its transformed or disguised state.
  • 交会 - Literally 'meet and interact,' used here to describe a sexual encounter.
  • 归宁 - Traditional term meaning 'return to one's parents' home' after marriage, typically for a short visit.
  • 昂首张口 - Literally 'raises head and opens mouth,' describing the snake's threatening posture.
  • 疾追及 - Literally 'chases swiftly and catches up,' emphasizing the snake's speed and persistence.
  • 绕而淫之 - Literally 'wraps around and has intercourse with,' describing the snake's actions.
  • 宛转不得脱 - Literally 'twists and turns, unable to escape,' depicting the woman's struggle.
  • 叫呼求救 - Literally 'shouts for help,' describing her desperate cry for assistance.
  • 莫能措手 - Literally 'cannot take action,' indicating the helplessness of the onlookers.
  • 尽夜至旦乃去 - Literally 'until dawn,' describing the duration of the event.
  • 举尾褰裳 - Literally 'lifts tail and raises skirt,' describing the snake's movements during the encounter.
  • 其捷如手 - Literally 'its agility is like a hand,' emphasizing the snake's quick and precise movements.
  • 裳皆破裂 - Literally 'skirts are all torn,' describing the physical damage to the woman's clothing.
  • 淫接甚久 - Literally 'intercourse lasts for a long time,' emphasizing the duration of the encounter.
  • 讶饷不至 - Literally 'surprised that the meal did not arrive,' indicating the husband's confusion and concern.
  • 举首怒目 - Literally 'raises head and glares,' describing the snake's threatening posture in the presence of the crowd.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蛇妖 - In Chinese folklore, snakes are often associated with supernatural powers and can transform into human form to deceive or harm people.
  • 归宁 - A traditional practice in Chinese culture where a married woman visits her parents' home, usually for a short period.
  • 建炎间 - Refers to the Jianyan period (1127-1130) during the Southern Song Dynasty, a time of political turmoil and conflict.
  • 南城县 - A county in Jiangxi Province, China, mentioned in the text.
  • 壕口宝慈观 - A specific location in the text, likely a temple or shrine in the area.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '蛇最能为妖' is straightforward but emphasizes the snake's supernatural abilities.
  • The phrase '化形魅人' is clear but requires careful translation to convey the idea of transformation and enchantment.
  • The phrase '真形亲与妇女交会' is elliptical and must be expanded to clarify the relationship between the snake and the woman.
  • The sentence '民家少妇因归宁' is clear but requires attention to the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '闻林中有声' is clear but requires attention to the setting and the woman's reaction.
  • The sentence '回顾、见大蛇在后' is clear but requires attention to the sequence of actions.
  • The sentence '妇惊走' is clear but requires attention to the woman's emotional state.
  • The sentence '蛇昂首张口' is clear but requires attention to the snake's threatening posture.
  • The sentence '疾追及' is clear but requires attention to the snake's speed and persistence.
  • The sentence '绕而淫之' is clear but requires attention to the snake's actions and the woman's struggle.
  • The sentence '妇宛转不得脱' is clear but requires attention to the woman's physical and emotional state.
  • The sentence '叫呼求救' is clear but requires attention to the woman's desperate cry for help.
  • The sentence '见者奔告其家' is clear but requires attention to the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '邻里皆来赴' is clear but requires attention to the community's response.
  • The sentence '莫能措手' is clear but requires attention to the helplessness of the onlookers.
  • The sentence '尽夜至旦乃去' is clear but requires attention to the duration of the event.
  • The sentence '又壕口宝慈观侧田家胡氏妇' is clear but requires attention to the specific location and the woman's background.
  • The sentence '春月饷田' is clear but requires attention to the seasonal context and the woman's activity.
  • The sentence '去家数里' is clear but requires attention to the distance and the setting.
  • The sentence '负担行山麓' is clear but requires attention to the woman's activity and the setting.
  • The sentence '过丛薄中' is clear but requires attention to the setting and the woman's movement.
  • The sentence '蛇追之' is clear but requires attention to the snake's actions and the woman's reaction.
  • The sentence '妇弃担走' is clear but requires attention to the woman's actions and the setting.
  • The sentence '未百步' is clear but requires attention to the distance and the woman's state.
  • The sentence '惊颤而仆' is clear but requires attention to the woman's physical and emotional state.
  • The sentence '为所及' is clear but requires attention to the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '以身匝绕' is clear but requires attention to the snake's actions and the woman's struggle.
  • The sentence '举尾褰裳' is clear but requires attention to the snake's movements and the woman's physical state.
  • The sentence '其捷如手' is clear but requires attention to the snake's agility and the woman's struggle.
  • The sentence '裳皆破裂' is clear but requires attention to the physical damage to the woman's clothing.
  • The sentence '淫接甚久' is clear but requires attention to the duration of the encounter.
  • The sentence '其夫讶饷不至' is clear but requires attention to the husband's reaction and the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '归就食' is clear but requires attention to the husband's actions and the setting.
  • The sentence '至则见之' is clear but requires attention to the husband's discovery and the sequence of events.
  • The sentence '愤恚不知所出' is clear but requires attention to the husband's emotional state and his actions.
  • The sentence '呼数十人持杖来救' is clear but requires attention to the community's response and the husband's actions.
  • The sentence '蛇对众举首怒目' is clear but requires attention to the snake's threatening posture and the community's reaction.

Proper Nouns

  • 南城县 - A county in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 壕口宝慈观 - A specific location in the text, likely a temple or shrine in the area.
  • 胡氏妇 - Refers to a woman named Hu, indicating her family name.

[1251]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呀口吐气 - A vivid, onomatopoeic expression describing a sudden, forceful exhalation, possibly indicating a display of power or intimidation.
  • 蓬勃如烟 - Describes something vigorous and expansive, like smoke spreading out, likely referring to the appearance or effect of the creature.
  • 众股栗莫敢前 - A hyperbolic expression meaning that everyone trembled with fear and dared not approach, emphasizing the creature's intimidating presence.
  • 熟视远伺 - Means to observe carefully from a distance, indicating cautious behavior.
  • 数日乃去 - Indicates that the creature stayed for several days before leaving.
  • 形肿腹胀 - Describes a swollen body and bloated abdomen, likely referring to the woman's physical condition.
  • 津沫狼藉 - Means saliva and mucus were scattered everywhere, indicating a messy and unpleasant scene.
  • 舁归下五色汁斗余 - Describes carrying the woman back home after she excreted several liters of colored liquid, emphasizing the severity of her condition.
  • 病逾年色如蜡 - Indicates that the woman fell ill for over a year and her complexion became as pale as wax, suggesting a prolonged and severe illness.
  • 刺绣开牕 - Means to embroider while opening the window, indicating a domestic activity.
  • 咽间有声鸣其傍 - Describes a sound coming from the creature's throat, possibly indicating a call or warning.
  • 伺左右无人 - Means to wait until no one was around, indicating secretive or cautious behavior.
  • 疾走入室 - Describes rushing into the room, likely in a hurry or with urgency.
  • 径就女为淫 - Means to immediately proceed to have sexual intercourse with the woman, indicating a direct and forceful action.
  • 引首搭肩上 - Describes the creature placing its head on the woman's shoulder, likely indicating an intimate or affectionate gesture.
  • 啼呼宛转不忍闻 - Means the woman cried and moaned in a way that was unbearable to hear, indicating her distress and pain.
  • 交讫乃去 - Indicates that the creature left after completing the act.
  • 遂妊娠 - Means she became pregnant, indicating the result of the encounter.
  • 十月产蜿蜒数十 - Describes giving birth to dozens of wriggling creatures, likely referring to snakes or snake-like beings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宜黄县 - Refers to Yihuang County, Nancheng, Jiangxi Province.
  • 南丰县 - Refers to Nancheng County, Nancheng, Jiangxi Province.
  • 绍兴丁丑岁 - Refers to the year 1137 AD, during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 董氏妇 - Refers to a woman from the Dong family.
  • 黑衣男子 - Refers to a man in black clothing, likely a supernatural or transformed being.
  • 野合 - Refers to an illicit or extramarital sexual encounter.
  • 异物 - Refers to a supernatural or transformed being.

Proper Nouns

  • 宜黄县 - Yihuang County, Nancheng, Jiangxi Province.
  • 南丰县 - Nancheng County, Nancheng, Jiangxi Province.
  • 绍兴丁丑岁 - The year 1137 AD, during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 董氏妇 - A woman from the Dong family.
  • 黑衣男子 - A man in black clothing.

[1252]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逾墙推户 - Climb over the wall and force open the door, describing how the intruder enters the house.
  • 强我与交 - Force me into intimacy or compel me to engage in sexual relations.
  • 感孕 - Becoming pregnant due to supernatural means.
  • 异雏 - Strange offspring or unusual child, referring to the unusual birth.
  • 化形 - Change form or shape-shift, referring to the ability of supernatural beings to transform.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 临川县曹舍村 - Cao She Village in Lichuan County, a specific location in China.
  • 南城竹油村 - Zhu You Village in Nancheng, a specific location in China.
  • 山鬼 - Mountain spirit or demon, often associated with dangerous or malevolent forces in Chinese folklore.
  • 异雏 - Supernatural or unusual birth, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 临川县 - Lichuan County, a specific location in China.
  • 曹舍村 - Cao She Village, a specific village in Lichuan County.
  • 南城 - Nancheng, a specific location in China.
  • 竹油村 - Zhu You Village, a specific village in Nancheng.

[1253]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 红叶入怀 - Literal expression meaning 'red leaf enters the bosom,' used metaphorically to describe an unusual or supernatural event.
  • 冉冉入怀 - Describes the red leaf entering the bosom in a gentle, slow manner.
  • 鲜红可爱 - Means 'bright red and lovely.'
  • 恍惚间 - Means 'in a daze' or 'in a dreamlike state.'
  • 言语错乱 - Means 'speech is confused' or 'speech is incoherent.'
  • 被发裸跣 - Means 'with hair loose and barefoot,' describing a state of disarray or madness.
  • 医巫无所施其伎 - Means 'neither doctors nor magicians can apply their skills,' indicating the inability to treat the condition.
  • 了不知何物为妖也 - Means 'completely unaware of what is causing the disturbance,' expressing the lack of understanding of the cause.
  • 杨氏灶神 - Refers to the kitchen god of the Yang family.
  • 天寒无所向 - Means 'cold and having nowhere to go.'
  • 藉草而寝 - Means 'lying down on grass to sleep.'
  • 霜重月明 - Describes a scene with heavy frost and bright moonlight.
  • 寒不得寐 - Means 'cold and unable to sleep.'
  • 忽一虎跃而来 - Means 'suddenly a tiger leaps forward.'
  • 翼从数鬼 - Means 'with several ghosts following.'
  • 皆伥也 - Means 'all are伥,' where '伥' refers to a type of ghost or spirit.
  • 直趋屋所 - Means 'directly to the house.'
  • 取草鼓舞为戏 - Means 'to take grass and dance for fun.'
  • 子不敢喘 - Means 'the son dared not breathe.'
  • 俄黑云劲风 - Means 'suddenly dark clouds and strong winds.'
  • 咫尺翳暝 - Means 'darkness in the distance.'
  • 虎若被物逐 - Means 'the tiger seems to be chased by something.'
  • 众伥亦散 - Means 'the ghosts also disperse.'
  • 命唤土地神 - Means 'command to call the land god.'
  • 汝受杨氏祭祀有年矣 - Means 'you have received the Yang family's sacrifices for many years.'
  • 公纵虎为暴 - Means 'you let the tiger become violent.'
  • 郎君几为所食 - Means 'the young master almost got eaten.'
  • 致烦吾出神兵驱之 - Means 'causing me to send out divine soldiers to drive them away.'
  • 汝可谓不职矣 - Means 'you can be called derelict in your duty.'
  • 吾乃其家灶君司命也 - Means 'I am the kitchen god of this house.'
  • 汝识乎 - Means 'do you recognize this?'
  • 土地谢罪而退 - Means 'the land god apologizes and retreats.'
  • 明日起视 - Means 'the next morning, look outside.'
  • 外有虎迹 - Means 'there are tiger tracks outside.'
  • 草皆散掷地上 - Means 'the grass is scattered on the ground.'
  • 后其父怒解 - Means 'later, the father's anger subsides.'
  • 子得归 - Means 'the son returns.'
  • 具言之 - Means 'tell all about it.'
  • 由是事灶益谨 - Means 'from then on, he serves the kitchen god more carefully.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红叶入怀 - A common motif in Chinese literature, often symbolizing love or fate.
  • 灶神 - The kitchen god, a deity in Chinese folklore who oversees the household and is believed to report to heaven on the family's behavior.
  • 伥 - A type of ghost or spirit in Chinese folklore, often associated with tigers and known for leading people astray.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨氏灶神 - Refers to the kitchen god of the Yang family.
  • 土地神 - Refers to the land god.

[1254]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 得宜春尉以死 - Died as the magistrate of Yichun.
  • 先以岁饥速售 - Sold his land quickly due to famine.
  • 产去而税存 - The property was sold, but the taxes remained.
  • 阅十余年 - After more than ten years.
  • 负官物至多 - Owed a large amount of government property.
  • 邑令李鼎治逋峻 - The county magistrate Li Dǐng enforced the collection of overdue taxes strictly.
  • 系姚子于狱累月 - Kept Yao's son in prison for several months.
  • 会岁尽 - When the year ended.
  • 使召保任 - Ordered to call in a guarantor.
  • 立期暂归 - Set a deadline for temporary return.
  • 独持饭一器祀其父 - Held a single bowl of rice to honor his father.
  • 告以久囚不能输税之故 - Told him about being imprisoned for a long time and unable to pay the taxes.
  • 哀号不已 - Continued to weep in sorrow.
  • 屋上忽有人呼小名 - Suddenly someone called his nickname from the roof.
  • 惊视之 - Looked up in surprise.
  • 父衣公服立 - His father stood in official attire.
  • 索纸墨笔砚 - Asked for paper, ink, brush, and inkstone.
  • 子欲梯而上 - The son wanted to climb up a ladder.
  • 第置四物檐间可也 - Just place the four items on the eaves.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姚师文 - A historical figure who served as the magistrate of Yichun and died in office.
  • 宜春 - The location where姚师文 served as magistrate.
  • 岁饥 - Refers to a period of famine, which was a common occurrence in ancient China and often led to economic hardship.
  • 邑令 - Refers to the local magistrate or county official, who had significant power over the administration and law enforcement in their jurisdiction.
  • 李鼎 - The name of the county magistrate who enforced strict measures to collect overdue taxes.
  • 保任 - A system where a guarantor would vouch for someone's ability to fulfill their obligations, often used in legal and financial contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 姚师文 - The name of the historical figure who served as the magistrate of Yichun and died in office.
  • 宜春 - The location where姚师文 served as magistrate.
  • 李鼎 - The name of the county magistrate who enforced strict measures to collect overdue taxes.

[1255]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忍泪屏息遥望之 - Holding back tears and holding one's breath to gaze afar; conveys deep emotion and anticipation.
  • 姚稍步及檐坐 - Gradually walking up to the eaves and sitting; suggests a slow, deliberate movement.
  • 就膝书满纸掷下 - Writing a full sheet of paper and throwing it down; indicates a sudden, forceful action.
  • 俯拾之际 - When bending to pick up; suggests a moment of action.
  • 父遂不见 - Then not seen; indicates the father's sudden disappearance.
  • 新岁持死父书至邑 - In the new year, holding the letter of the deceased father to the town; describes the son bringing the letter to the town.
  • 邑宰读所书 - The magistrate of the town reading what was written; indicates the magistrate reading the letter.
  • 某田归某家 - A certain field belonging to a certain family; describes the transfer of property.
  • 税当若干 - Tax should be a certain amount; specifies the tax amount.
  • 逮逋人至 - To apprehend a fugitive to come; describes the arrival of a fugitive.
  • 皆骇异承伏 - All shocked and surprised, admitting and submitting; indicates the fugitives' reaction.
  • 子乃得免 - The son then escaped; indicates the son's escape.
  • 子妇之父董在临川 - The son's wife's father Dong is in Linchuan; introduces the father-in-law.
  • 素相善 - Always friendly; indicates a long-standing friendship.
  • 空中揖语 - In the air, bowing and speaking; describes a ghostly interaction.
  • 相劳如平生 - Consoling each other as if in life; indicates a conversation as if they were alive.
  • 且请具酒席 - And asked to prepare a feast; describes a request for a feast.
  • □□而不见形 - Even though or as if not; indicates that despite the request, the father did not appear.
  • 以何礼为席 - With what etiquette to prepare the table; asks about the proper etiquette for the feast.
  • 与生人等耳 - The same as living people; indicates that the etiquette should be the same as for living people.
  • 董如言 - Dong followed the instructions; indicates that Dong followed the instructions.
  • 相对尽敬 - Facing each other, showing utmost respect; describes the respectful interaction.
  • 不敢少慢 - Did not dare to be slightly slow or negligent; indicates that they did not dare to be negligent.
  • 又语及教子 - And spoke about teaching the son; indicates a discussion about educating the son.
  • 为出论题 - To propose topics for discussion; describes the proposal of discussion topics.
  • 说题意主张有条理 - To explain the meaning of the topic, having a clear and organized approach; indicates a clear and organized discussion.
  • 罢酒 - To finish drinking; indicates the end of the feast.
  • 始辞去 - Then bid farewell and leave; indicates the departure.
  • 仍嘱善护其子 - Still instructed to take good care of the son; indicates a final instruction.
  • 自此寂然 - From this point, quietly; indicates that everything became quiet after this event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 忍泪屏息遥望之 - Reflects a traditional Chinese value of emotional restraint and anticipation.
  • 姚稍步及檐坐 - The act of gradually walking up to the eaves and sitting suggests a ritual or a moment of significance.
  • 就膝书满纸掷下 - The sudden writing and throwing of a full sheet of paper indicates a moment of intense emotion or urgency.
  • 父遂不见 - The father's sudden disappearance is a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature, often symbolizing a supernatural event.
  • 新岁持死父书至邑 - The son bringing the letter to the town in the new year suggests a time of reflection and remembrance.
  • 邑宰读所书 - The magistrate reading the letter indicates the importance of official documents and the role of local governance.
  • 某田归某家 - The transfer of property is a significant legal and social issue in traditional Chinese society.
  • 税当若干 - The specification of the tax amount reflects the importance of taxation in maintaining the state.
  • 逮逋人至 - The apprehension of a fugitive highlights the role of law enforcement in maintaining order.
  • 皆骇异承伏 - The shocked and surprised reaction of the fugitives indicates the power of the letter and the authority of the deceased father.
  • 子乃得免 - The son's escape suggests a miraculous or supernatural intervention.
  • 子妇之父董在临川 - The introduction of the father-in-law suggests a network of relationships and alliances.
  • 素相善 - The long-standing friendship indicates the importance of social bonds in traditional Chinese society.
  • 空中揖语 - The ghostly interaction reflects the belief in the afterlife and the possibility of communication with the dead.
  • 相劳如平生 - The conversation as if they were alive suggests a deep emotional connection and the persistence of relationships beyond death.
  • 且请具酒席 - The request for a feast indicates a desire to honor and remember the deceased.
  • □□而不见形 - The missing phrase suggests a moment of uncertainty or a supernatural event.
  • 以何礼为席 - The question about the proper etiquette for the feast reflects the importance of ritual and tradition.
  • 与生人等耳 - The instruction to follow the same etiquette as for living people suggests a continuity of social norms.
  • 董如言 - The following of instructions indicates respect and obedience.
  • 相对尽敬 - The respectful interaction reflects the importance of propriety and respect in social interactions.
  • 不敢少慢 - The lack of negligence indicates a high level of respect and attention.
  • 又语及教子 - The discussion about educating the son suggests the importance of education and family values.
  • 为出论题 - The proposal of discussion topics indicates a structured and organized approach to learning.
  • 说题意主张有条理 - The clear and organized discussion reflects the importance of logical and coherent reasoning.
  • 罢酒 - The end of the feast indicates the conclusion of the ritual.
  • 始辞去 - The departure indicates the end of the interaction.
  • 仍嘱善护其子 - The final instruction to take good care of the son suggests a concern for the future and the well-being of the family.
  • 自此寂然 - The quiet aftermath suggests a return to normalcy and a sense of closure.

[1256]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 徐以清缺 - Phrase meaning 'slowly and clearly fill the vacancy' or 'gradually fill the vacancy with clarity.'
  • 内文缺 - Refers to an internal vacancy or position within an organization or institution.
  • 就馆于村墅 - Means 'to stay in a village inn or guesthouse.'
  • 中道如厕 - Means 'to stop on the road to use the restroom.'
  • 负租坐系 - Refers to being imprisoned due to unpaid rent.
  • 特无所从出 - Means 'especially without any means to pay.'
  • 随身赍挟 - Means 'to carry with oneself.'
  • 谨有此数 - Means 'carefully have this amount.'
  • 悉与之 - Means 'to give it all to him.'
  • 岁夕无以祭神 - Means 'on the eve of the year, unable to offer sacrifices to the gods.'
  • 不悔也 - Means 'without regret.'
  • 累举恩 - Refers to being repeatedly recommended or rewarded for one's merits.
  • 至承议郎 - Means 'to reach the rank of Chengyi Lang' (承议郎).
  • 生五子 - Means 'to have five sons.'
  • 京至国子司业 - Refers to Zhu Jing, who reached the position of Guozijiaoshi (国子司业).
  • 彦终待制 - Refers to Zhu Yan, who ultimately became a Dazhi (待制).
  • 褒为郎官 - Refers to Zhu Bao, who became a Langguan (郎官).
  • 襄至郡守 - Refers to Zhu Xiang, who became a Junshou (郡守).
  • 皆知名当世 - Means 'all were well-known in their time.'
  • 清健康宁 - Refers to Zhu Qing, who was healthy and peaceful.
  • 及见诸子达官 - Means 'until he saw his sons achieve high ranks.'
  • 享甘旨 - Means 'to enjoy delicious food and fine wine.'
  • 年八十有余乃卒 - Means 'at the age of over eighty, he passed away.'
  • 里中人至今能言之 - Means 'the people in the village can still talk about it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朱承议 - A title in the Chinese bureaucracy, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 南丰朱氏 - Refers to the Zhu family from Nanfeng, a place in Jiangxi Province.
  • 国子司业 - A high-ranking official in the imperial academy, responsible for education.
  • 待制 - A title given to officials who were on standby for imperial service.
  • 郎官 - A general term for officials in the imperial administration.
  • 郡守 - Refers to the governor of a prefecture or county.
  • 累举恩 - Refers to the practice of repeatedly recommending or rewarding individuals for their merits, a common practice in the imperial examination system.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱承议 - A title or name, likely referring to a person with the title 'Zhu Chengyi' (朱承议).
  • 南丰朱氏 - Refers to the Zhu family from Nanfeng, a place in Jiangxi Province.
  • 承议郎 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 国子司业 - A high-ranking official in the imperial academy, responsible for education.
  • 待制 - A title given to officials who were on standby for imperial service.
  • 郎官 - A general term for officials in the imperial administration.
  • 郡守 - Refers to the governor of a prefecture or county.

[1257]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 巴山蛇 - Title of the text, likely referring to a specific legend or story involving a snake from Mount Ba.
  • 暴衣 - Means 'to dry clothes' or 'to expose clothes to the sun.'

  • 明旦雪深尺余 - Describes the next morning when the snow was several inches deep.
  • 雪中迹如兵马所经 - Describes the tracks in the snow as if they were made by soldiers.
  • 人畜鸟兽之踪相半 - Describes the tracks of people, animals, birds, and beasts mixed together.
  • 或流血污染 - Describes some of the tracks being stained with blood.
  • 如此几十许里 - Describes the tracks extending for dozens of miles.
  • 入深山乃绝 - Means 'they ended in the deep mountains.'
  • 自十八卷至此 - Refers to the beginning of the text, possibly indicating a larger work.
  • 除路当可一事外 - Means 'except for one thing that could be considered on the road.'
  • 皆建昌士人邓植端若转为予言 - Means 'all of these were told to me by the scholar Deng Zhidian of Jianchang.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍兴庚午岁十一月 - Refers to the year 1150 AD, during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 建昌新城县永安村 - A specific location in the Jianchang region, which is now part of modern-day Jiangxi Province.
  • 绍兴 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 庚午 - A stem-branch combination used in the Chinese calendar system.
  • 风雪大作 - A common weather phenomenon in northern China during winter.
  • 鬼迹 - The term 'ghost tracks' is often used in Chinese literature to describe mysterious or supernatural occurrences.

Proper Nouns

  • 绍兴 - The reign title of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 庚午 - A stem-branch combination used in the Chinese calendar system.
  • 建昌 - A historical region in Jiangxi Province.
  • 新城县 - A county in the Jianchang region.
  • 永安村 - A village in the Jianchang region.
  • 邓植端 - A scholar from Jianchang, who provided the information.
